firmware.summary:
Few quality of life updates, fixed some drawing boxes that were causing font issues on victron shunt


Added a timer to menu, to reduce the updates speed to Battery / Shunt screen. This might reduce the flicker causes by having the screen update so much.

Added support for Mopeka wireless tank sensor - Untested, but hopefully it will work.




Victron Smart BatteryProtect
Victron Lynx Smart BMS
Victron DC Energy Meter
Victron Smart Battery Sense
Fogstar Drift Bluetooth Battery
Roamer Bluetooth Battery

Eco-Worthy/JBD battery scanning has been improved.
Fogstar Drift and Roamer battery pages now keep their own separate data/status rather than sharing Eco-Worthy’s display state.
firmware.changes:
Added a timer to menu, to reduce the updates speed to Battery / Shunt screen. This might reduce the flicker causes by having the screen update so much.

Added support for Mopeka wireless tank sensor - Untested, but hopefully it will work.




A lot has changed since beta 6.14, so here is a quick summary of what has been added and improved.
The biggest change is that ChargeScreen now supports quite a few more optional screens. These can be turned on or off from the WiFi setup page, so you only need to show the devices you actually use.
New screens added since beta 6.14:
Victron Smart BatteryProtect
Victron Lynx Smart BMS
Victron DC Energy Meter
Victron Smart Battery Sense
Fogstar Drift Bluetooth Battery
Roamer Bluetooth Battery
The Victron screens use Instant Readout, so each one has its own key box in the WiFi setup page. The Fogstar and Roamer screens are experimental Bluetooth battery screens using the same JBD-style battery support already used for Eco-Worthy batteries.
There have also been some setup changes. New devices now start with Demo Mode on, so you can scroll through all available screens straight away and see what ChargeScreen can display. When Demo Mode is turned off, the device goes back to showing only the Victron SmartShunt / BMV Battery Monitor screen unless other screens have been enabled in WiFi setup.
The WiFi settings page has also been tidied up. Victron devices are grouped together, device names are clearer, and the setup guide has been simplified so users can follow it step by step.
A couple of important fixes are included too:
Victron Smart Battery Sense should no longer be mistaken for a SmartShunt.
Eco-Worthy/JBD battery scanning has been improved.
Fogstar Drift and Roamer battery pages now keep their own separate data/status rather than sharing Eco-Worthy’s display state.
As always, the newer battery types may need real-world testing. If a Fogstar or Roamer battery does not show data, the best next step is to use the BLE Capture option from the WiFi page and send over the capture file so support can be tightened up.

instructions.power: Power ChargeScreen using USB-C or the rear connector. If using the rear connector, make sure to line up the notch at the top of the cable with the gap on the plug.
instructions.victron_intro: ChargeScreen reads Victron Instant Readout Bluetooth adverts. Those adverts are encrypted, so each Victron device needs its own Instant Readout encryption data.
instructions.victron_first_step: Open the VictronConnect app near your Victron Shunt or Victron MPPT solar controller.
instructions.ble_intro: BLE capture records nearby Bluetooth Low Energy adverts for 5 minutes. It is useful when adding support for batteries or accessories that do not decode correctly yet.
instructions.ble_privacy: Privacy note: the CSV may include packets from nearby watches, phones, TVs, sensors, and batteries. Most of it is encrypted or only useful to the original device, but it will still appear in the file. Battery encryption codes are only used to decode the battery packets in the capture.

purchase.text: ChargeScreen units are available premade and supplied with the firmware already installed. Visit the T6 Forum project thread for availability, discussion, and ordering details.
